methods.method_name: Bee Nest Setup and Larvae Allocation
methods.description: This method describes the setup of experimental nests for Osmia bicornis, the release and breeding of adults, and the allocation of larvae to treatment groups with different diets.

steps.description: Diapausing O. bicornis cocoons were released in June 2019 inside experimental nests in a south-facing location on the University of Hull campus, and adults allowed to emerge and breed.
steps.searchable_summary: release O. bicornis cocoons in experimental nests for breeding

steps.description: The nests consisted of styrofoam blocks with a groove cut out of the surface and covered with a transparent acrylic slide for observation, housed within custom-built outdoor wooden boxes.
steps.searchable_summary: construct and set up observation nests for bees

steps.description: Completed nests were brought into the laboratory. When larvae were old enough to be handled, they were each weighed and then placed into a single-cell nest following the removal of any egg fragments.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh larvae and place into single-cell nests

steps.description: Within this nest each larva was randomly allocated to a treatment group and provided with one of the five experimental diets.
steps.searchable_summary: allocate larvae to treatment groups with different diets

steps.description: All larvae were placed into an environmental chamber at 19.5°C and 75% humidity.

methods.description: This method outlines the preparation of different experimental diets for the larvae, including the use of pollen and various diluents.

steps.description: The pollen used in all of the diets was honey bee pollen obtained in bulk and homogenized to ensure consistency across pollen balls.
steps.searchable_summary: homogenize bulk honey bee pollen for diet consistency

steps.description: The full pollen ball treatment (diet A) weighed 0.35g, close to the largest field pollen ball mass recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare full pollen ball treatment weighing 0.35g

steps.description: The reduced pollen ball treatment (diet B) was identical in composition but 70% of the mass, i.e. 0.245 g.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare reduced pollen ball treatment weighing 0.245g

steps.description: In the diluted diets, the diluents used were 3% bacteriological agar, whole, empty pollen exines, and pollen exines crushed in a ball mill.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare diluted diets with various diluents

methods.description: This method involves assessing the survival rate of larvae, the time to death for non-surviving larvae, and the latency to spin cocoons.

steps.description: We assessed the proportion of larvae surviving the experiment, up to a maximum of 70 days.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate survival rate of larvae over 70 days

steps.description: We assessed the time to death for non-surviving larvae.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze time to death for non-surviving larvae

steps.description: We assessed the latency to spin cocoons during that period.
We assessed final cocoon weight by weighing cocoons 21 days after the date larvae started spinning.
steps.searchable_summary: weigh cocoons 21 days after spinning starts
